title: Festivals Celebrated by the Indian Community
description: Major Indian festivals and cultural events celebrated across Belgium.

items.name: Diwali
items.description: Festival of lights, celebrated with community events at temples such as Brussels Mandir and Ram Mandir, and among Antwerp's Gujarati community.

items.name: Holi
items.description: Festival of colours, celebrated with community gatherings when weather permits, popular among students in university cities.

items.name: Indian Independence Day
items.description: Flag hoisting and cultural events, typically held at or in coordination with the Embassy of India in Brussels.

items.name: Navratri / Garba
items.description: Nine nights of dance and celebration, especially popular within the Gujarati diamond-trading community in Antwerp; Durga Jaagran is also marked at Brussels Mandir.

items.name: Vaisakhi
items.description: Sikh harvest and new year festival, celebrated at Gurdwara Sangat Sahib in Sint-Truiden (Belgium's largest gurudwara) and Gurdwara Guru Nanak Sahib in the Brussels area.

items.name: Gent India Dans Festival (GIDF)
items.description: An annual festival of Indian dance in Ghent curated by Swapnil Dagliya and organised by ABC – A Bollywood Company, featuring performances and workshops.
